svg.title: Sequence diagram: lifespan vs dependency injection for Redis in FastAPI
svg.desc: Shows two phases: app startup creating the pool via lifespan, then per-request dependency injection retrieving it
svg.text: Phase 1 — app startup (once, before any request)
Phase 2 — per request (runs for every HTTP call)
Lifespan: pool created ONCE, stored in app.state. Lives for entire process lifetime.
Dependency: called on EVERY request. Only reads app.state — no pool creation, no new connections.
Without lifespan → no pool to read. Without dep injection → no clean way to pass pool into routes.
Phase 3 — app shutdown: lifespan resumes after yield → pool.aclose() called once
